UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's public profile data within the EidSpark application, linked to their authentication system ID. This entity stores non-authentication-sensitive user information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, corresponding to the external authentication system's user ID.

displayName.description: The user's preferred display name for public interaction.

email.description: The user's email address, used for contact or non-authentication-critical purposes.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

Mosque.title: Mosque
Mosque.description: Represents a mosque with its geographical location and core details for the EidSpark's Live Eid Jamaat Finder.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Mosque entity.

name.description: The official name of the mosque.

district.description: The administrative district in Bangladesh where the mosque is located (e.g., 'Dhaka District').

area.description: A more specific local area or neighborhood within the district (e.g., 'Gulshan', 'Mirpur').

latitude.description: The geographical latitude coordinate of the mosque's location for map display.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude coordinate of the mosque's location for map display.

googleMapsLink.description: A URL to the mosque's location on Google Maps for directions.

description.description: An optional note or additional details about the mosque, often submitted by users.

isApprovedByAdmin.description: Indicates whether the mosque's core data has been reviewed and approved by an administrator.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the mosque entry was first submitted.

submittedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initially submitted this mosque entry. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Mosque)

EidJamaatTime.title: EidJamaatTime
EidJamaatTime.description: Represents a specific Eid prayer (Jamaat) time slot for a given mosque on a particular Eid date, allowing for multiple times per mosque and community verification.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EidJamaatTime entity.

mosqueId.description: Reference to the Mosque entity this prayer time belongs to. (Relationship: Mosque 1:N EidJamaatTime)

eidDate.description: The specific date of the Eid for which this prayer time is scheduled (e.g., '2023-04-21' for Eid al-Fitr).

time.description: The specific time of the Eid Jamaat (e.g., '07:00 AM', '08:30 AM').

submittedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this specific prayer time entry was submitted.

submittedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who submitted this prayer time. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N EidJamaatTime)

isApprovedByAdmin.description: Indicates whether this specific prayer time entry has been reviewed and approved by an administrator.

communitySubmissionCount.description: A count of how many distinct users have submitted this exact prayer time for this mosque and Eid date, contributing to community verification. A higher count implies more verification.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile data. Access is private, restricted to the authenticated user matching the 'userId' in the path. The 'userId' parameter corresponds to `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Identifies users with administrative privileges. The mere existence of a document at this path for a specific 'userId' grants admin access. This follows the Database Access Control (DBAC) pattern where roles are stored in the database. Read access is public for security rule evaluation; write access is restricted to existing admins or privileged backend functions.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who possesses administrative privileges.

definition.description: Stores core details about each mosque. Publicly readable if `isApprovedByAdmin` is true. Authenticated users can create new mosque entries. Only the `submittedByUserId` or an admin can update/delete the entry. Includes denormalized `submittedByUserId` for authorization independence and explicit ownership.

params.name: mosqueId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific mosque.

definition.description: Stores specific Eid prayer times for a mosque. Publicly readable if its own `isApprovedByAdmin` is true. Authenticated users can create new prayer time entries or increment `communitySubmissionCount` for existing ones. Only the `submittedByUserId` or an admin can update/delete the entry. Includes denormalized `mosqueId` (for querying and independence) and `submittedByUserId` (for submitter ownership checks). The `isApprovedByAdmin` field on this document enables Authorization Independence for read operations, removing reliance on the parent mosque's approval status in rules.

params.name: mosqueId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent mosque to which this prayer time belongs.

params.name: jamaatTimeId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific Eid prayer time slot.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for the 'Live Eid Jamaat Finder' feature is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

1.  **Authorization Independence & Denormalization:**
    *   **Mosque Approval (`isApprovedByAdmin`):** Both `Mosque` documents in `/mosques/{mosqueId}` and `EidJamaatTime` documents in `/mosques/{mosqueId}/jamaatTimes/{jamaatTimeId}` include their own `isApprovedByAdmin` boolean field. This is critical for Authorization Independence. Public read access to a specific `EidJamaatTime` document can be granted solely based on its own `isApprovedByAdmin` field, without requiring a `get()` operation on the parent `Mosque` document. Similarly, the `Mosque` document itself is publicly readable only if its `isApprovedByAdmin` is true. This eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, enabling atomic operations (like creating a mosque and its first jamaat time in a batch) and simplifying security rules significantly.
    *   **Ownership (`submittedByUserId`):** Each `Mosque` and `EidJamaatTime` document contains `submittedByUserId`. This denormalizes the ownership context directly into the document, allowing `request.auth.uid` checks for edit/delete permissions by the original submitter without cross-document lookups.
    *   **`mosqueId` in `EidJamaatTime`:** The `mosqueId` is explicitly included in each `EidJamaatTime` document. While a subcollection path inherently knows its parent's ID, this explicit field aids in querying and also supports authorization independence by allowing rules to potentially check against the parent's ID if needed for specific logic (though the `isApprovedByAdmin` on the `EidJamaatTime` itself handles public read independence).

2.  **Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
    *   **Public and Pending Data:** Instead of separating 'approved' and 'pending' mosques into different collections (which complicates listing and search), all `Mosque` and `EidJamaatTime` documents reside in their respective main collections (`/mosques` and `/mosques/{mosqueId}/jamaatTimes`). Access control is managed by the `isApprovedByAdmin` field. This ensures a homogeneous security posture within each collection: all documents *can* be publicly read if `isApprovedByAdmin: true`, or writable by the submitter/admin regardless of approval status. This greatly simplifies rules for QAPs (Queries as Permissions) as client applications can perform `list` operations with a `where('isApprovedByAdmin', '==', true)` filter, knowing that the rules will enforce this server-side.
    *   **Admin Roles:** A dedicated top-level collection, `/roles_admin/{userId}`, is used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The mere *existence* of a document in this collection for a given `userId` grants administrative privileges. This segregates role information from user profiles and provides an explicit, easily checked permission structure without custom claims.

3.  **QAPs (Queries as Permissions) & Scalability:**
    *   The top-level `/mosques` collection and its subcollection `/jamaatTimes` are designed for efficient public listing and searching. Users can query `/mosques` by `district`, `area`, or `name` and filter by `isApprovedByAdmin == true` to retrieve approved mosque data. Similarly, `jamaatTimes` for an approved mosque can be listed, also filtered by `isApprovedByAdmin == true`. This design inherently supports the search feature and map display without requiring complex, inefficient, or insecure security rules. Firebase security rules can directly enforce that only documents with `isApprovedByAdmin: true` are returned for public `list` queries, ensuring that rules are not used as filters after data retrieval. The `communitySubmissionCount` field on `EidJamaatTime` can be used for client-side ordering or additional filtering based on community verification status without impacting core security rules.
